Who in October eats beneath the āṅolā tree To Heaven will surely go with all his family. āṅwal gaṭṭā , n. m. A dried myrobalan, or one fallen from the tree.

Walī ko ṭuk dikhā sūrat apas kī, Khaṛā hai muntazir tere daras kā . Walī. To Wali just one moment show thy face, He waits expectant but to worship thee. 2. Yourself; Sir; His worship. Āp, āp haiṅ; aur, aur haiṅ; kyā āp se nisbat? Dāg. You are yourself, apart; others are but others! Compare them with you? 3. A term of respectful address to a gentleman, a superior, an equal, an elder, and also to a son or junior. Āp kā bandā, aur phire naṅgā! Āp kā naukar, aur khāë udhār! Gālib. Your slave, and naked wander, Sire! Your servant, and on credit live!

def: s.f. Self, yourself, themselves; each for him or herself; of himself, &c. ( = āp-se-āp). — āpā bisrānā, v.n. To forget, or lose consciousness of, self; to put aside self; to mortify the body. — āpā tajnā, v.n. To give up self; to sacrifice self; to give oneself up to grief, sorrow, suffering, &c.; to cast off self (i.e. the body), to commit suicide. — āpā-dhāpī, s.f. A race for self, eagerness to gratify self, selfishness. — āpā-dhāpī paṛnā, v.n. To pursue, or be intent on, the gratification of self, or the advancing of one's own interests. — āpam-dhāp = āpā-dhāp. — āpe-se bāhar honā, To be beside oneself (with joy or anger), to be in ecstacies; to be in a fury; to lose all control over self. — āpe-meṅ ānā, To come to oneself; to come to one's senses, to recover from a swoon, or from insensibility, or from a fit of anger, or from intoxication, or from fatigue.
more: n. m. 1. Self; I; the person speaking. 2. Oneself; yourself. Apne āpe ko dekho . Look to yourself. āpā bisrā'nā , v. n. To forget, or disregard self; to mortify the body. Brahm gyān hȳe dharo, bolte kā khoj karo, Māyā, agyāṅ haro, āpā bisrāo re . Kabīr. Brahm ever cherish in thy heart, search well thy soul. Throw off illusion, ignorance, and self forget. āpā taj'nā , v. n. 1. To give oneself up to grief, suffering, etc.; to mortify, or sacrifice oneself; not to care for oneself. 2. To commit suicide; to destroy oneself. Sunī sunāī bāt ke pīchhe tū ne apnā āpā taj dȳā . Wom. Kahānī . āpe se bāhar ho'nā , v. n. To be transported with rage or pleasure; to fall into a passion; to be unable to control oneself; to be beside oneself. 1. Āp hī āp, āpe se bāhar ho gayā , 2. Āpe se ho gayā hai kyoṅ bāhar? Āg lag jāë terī mastī par! Wom. Shauq. This transport, this excitement whence? May fires co…

def: n. f. 1. Self; selves; each for himself. 1. Āpā āpī sab hauāil hā, achchhā, pahle tūhīṅ lā, ham na leb . Mag. Wom. There, you care only for yourselves. (said in a pet). "Well, well, you go first. I won't have any." [Scene before a Bania 's shop. A crowd of women, returned from their day's labour, severally struggling to be served first.] 2. Santo, ghar meṅ jhagṛā bhārī, Rāti, devas, mil uṭhi uṭhi lāge, Pāṅch jhoṅṭā, ek nārī, Nyāro nyāro bhojan chāhe, Pāṅchoṅ adhik sawādī, Koū kāhū kī hata na māne, Apū hī āp Murārī . Kabīr. Saints! in this body great's the strife, All night and day they strive and strain, Five 1 youths, one 2 woman, 'mong them all, Each one desiring food for self, All five are thorough epicures, No one doth mind the other's nay, Each one is Lord all to himself. 2. Selfishness; self-love; self-seeking. PHR. A regard for number one. 1. Baṛī āpā dhāpī hai, jo hai, apnā-hī muṅh jhulastā hai . Wom. It is all one great rush for self, each one striving only to fill his own mouth.! (may it burn). 2. Sab meṅ āpā dhāpī hai . We are all for self. 3. Āpam dhāp kārā kar bīte. Jo pahle māre, so jīte . Prov. Self with self, clatter of arms, Who strikes first, wins. ā'pā-dhā pī paṛ'nā , v. n. To be deeply engaged in schemes or exertions in one's own interest. ā'pam-dhāpkar'nā , To look to one's own interest.

def: s.m. Wrong road; bad road; devious track; heresy.
more: ( अ and पंथाः A road) s. m. A wrong or bad road, a devious track; heresy.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having lost one's way; devious, going astray; heretical; — one who has lost his way, wanderer; heretic.
more: adj. One who has lost his way, devious; heretical.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Sinless, innocent, guiltless.
For compounds having āp for the first member, v.s.v. آپ āp.
more: pro. Each one; each by itself or himself; every one for himself. 1. Do purakh āp āp ko ṭhāḍe, Jab mileṅ, jab nit ke g̣āḍhe . Riddle. Ans. Kiwāṛ , door. Two males, each stands alone, apart. They meet, they're close knit heart to heart. 2. Gāoṅ meṅ āg lagī, sab āp āp ko bhāg gaë. āp āp kar' nā , v. a. 1. To address one with respect or deference. 2. To flatter; butter (Slang). Hamārā to āp āp karte mūṅh sūkh gayā, unkebhāveṅ naīṅ . I have flattered no end till my tongue is dry, but all to no purpose. āp bhātī , adv. Wom. According to one's own pleasure or choice. āp bītī , n. The story of one's own sufferings. Āp bītī kahūṅ, ki jag bītī? Prov. Shall I tell my own story, or another's? āp khurādī, āp murādī , n. Wom. Prov. Self-eating, self-regarding. 1. One who cares for number one only; a selfish or self-seeking person. 2. A free and independent person; one's own master.

def: adj. Shoreless; unbounded, boundless, interminable, infinite; excessive; impassable; unsurpassed; unrivalled.
more: n. m. lit . peeling off the skin. An irritating composition for peeling off the skin of the male organ, held by natives to be a cure for impotency. Upāṛ se sustī (impotency) jātī rahegī .

def: adj. (f. -ā), Another, other, a second; following; inferior; posterior; opposite; western. — aparaṅća, adv. Moreover.

adj. S. पृ to finish. 1. Boundless; infinite; without limits, or bounds. Terī māyā apār hai Ram ! 2. Inexpressible; beyond description. Jāë paṛe haiṅ Janak nagar meṅ, sobhā bhaī apār . 3. Intense; keen; excessive. Māgh mās meṅ, he sakhī, sardī paṛe apār. Ṭhaṅḍī pawan purwā chale, khāṅḍe kī sī dhār . Wom. Song. Bārā-māsā . In January, dear, the cold is most severe, The cutting East wind pierces like a sword.

s.m. Fault, crime, transgression, guilt, offence; injury, wrong, violence. — aparādh karnā (-kā), To commit an offence (against); to sin (against). — aparādh lagānā (-par, -ko), To impute a crime (to), to accuse, charge; to defame, slauder.
adj. (f. -ikā), Pertaining to guilt.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Guilty (of, -kā); faulty, offending; — offender, criminal, sinner.
more: adj. Vicious; corrupt; immoral; sinful. aprā'dhī , n. m. A bad man; an evil doer; a culprit; wrong-doer; transgressor; offender; criminal. Hīrāman ko dekh-ke, aprādhī hulsāë, Jā bāg ke bīch meṅ, to dīnā jāl bichhāë . Play. Song. Rūp Basant . No sooner does he see the parrot than the wicked man, Grows covetous, and forthwith in the orchard spreads his net.
s. m. A criminal, sinner, offender, transgressor.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit अपराधिन् (aparādhin, adjective); equivalent to اَپْرادھ (aprādh, “crime, offence”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s. f. A criminal, sinner, offender, transgressor.
s.m. The third watch of the day; the afternoon.
s. m. Not a defeat; victory, invincibleness.
s.m. Non-defeat; victory; invincibleness.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Not defeated; invincible; unerring.

more: s. f. The name of a twining shrub (called also کویل koyal, and کواٿهینٿهي kawwā-ṭhenṭhī, i. e. Crowʼs beak) Clitoria ternatea: also, the name of other plants.

more: s. m. 1. The state of a Hindū after bathing, previous to worship or to eating, during which it is unlawful for him to touch any one. 2. (for the Arabic ابرص) Leprosy.

def: s.f. Kindred, brotherhood, fraternity; fellowship
more: pro. Each other; one another; reciprocal; mutual. āpas, āpas-dārī , n. f. Fraternity; brotherhood; kindred. āpas-dārī kā māmla, āpas ka muāmla , n. m. A family or caste affair. āpas meṅ , adv. Among themselves; mutually; in concert; together. āpas meṅ rahn'ā , v. n. 1. To live together; to live together as man and wife. 2. To cohabit; to live in adultery. Ham kahāṅ, tum kahāṅ, yeh kahte haiṅ. Ki "ye āpas meṅ donoṅ rahte haiṅ ." Asar. Where I, where you! the story runs, they say, That we two with each other live alway. 3. To live in incest or the commission of unnatural offences. Yeh aurteṅ kisī mard ke pās nahīṅ jātīn, āpas meṅ rahtī haiṅ .
E. adj. Poor; helpless. Appas jíū ne kāhe māre he? Rus.

(see آپ āp ) pron.recip. Self, selves, oneʼs self, themselves, one another; (hence) s. f. Kindred, brotherhood, fellowship. آپس مین رهنا āpas meṅ rahnā, is applied to signify adultery, &c. (in the dialect of women).
subs: each other, one another. — āpas-dārī (see dārī), s.f. Kindred, &c. = āpas

and āpas-kā, adj. (f. -ī), Private, family (e.g. āpas-kā muʻāmlā, 'a private, or family, affair,' or 'an affair concerning our- or themselves'). — āpas-meṅ, adv. Among themselves, with each other or one another, together, mutually, in common, in concert
To live together; to live together as man and wife, to live in adultery; to live apart from men and in unnatural intercourse with one another (as certain women in India do).

def: s.m. Wind broken backwards; a disagreeable sound; ungrammatical language; an unmeaning or jingling word appended to another (e.g. wānī, in pānī wānī: — syn. تابع tābiʻ).
more: s. m. A disagreeable sound, wind broken backwards, ungrammatical language.

more: n. f. S. पद् dignity. 1. Loss of credit; disgrace; dishonor; insult. In patītan merī apat bichārī . Song. Sūr Dās. These villains on my ruin bent. 2. Calamity ( āfat ); misfortune; adversity. Dhīraj, dharm, mitr, aru nārī, Āpat kāl parakhye chārī . Rāmāyan. Patience, your faith, a friend, a woman, try These four when sharp adversity is nigh.

more: adj. One whose hands and feet are become useless; paralytick.

adj. (f. -ā), Defiling, polluting; impure.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Independent, absolute, uncontrolled.
adj. (f. -ā), Independent, absolute, uncontrolled.
adj. (f. -ā), Unclean, unholy; impure, defiled.

s.m. Destruction; death; injury; loss
s.m. Disrepute, disgrace, infamy.

pron. (dakh.) Self: see آپ āp.

(govern. gen. -kī), In reference (to), in respect (of), in comparison (with). — apekshā rakhnā (-kī), To hope (for); entertain the desire (of); to look (to), have need (of); to need, require. — apekshā karnā (-kī), To be in expectation or anticipation (of), to wait (for), to expect; to hope (for).

v.n. To swell out, be distended (the stomach); to be satiated, gorged, overloaded (the stomach); to become corpulent; to exceed bounds; to be puffed up with pride; to be unduly elated with success; to boast, swagger; to be or become very rich (cf. aphrā jānā)
more: (perhaps آپ Self, and भरण Filling) v. n. To swell out (the belly), to gormandize, to eat to satiety; to become very rich.
subs: v.n. To move or walk with a swaggering gait, to strut.

def: v. n. 1. To swell; bulge; fill; to be full; to be overloaded (the stomach), ready to burst. 1. Aphren bībī, de māreṅ . Riddle. Ans. roṭī (bread). The lady swells—down with her. 2. Māsh kī dāl se peṭ aphar gayā . 3. Jhūt na bole to peṭ aphar jāë . Prov. Lie he must, or he'll burst. 2. To grow fat or pot-bellied. Lālā gaddī pe baiṭhe baiṭhe aphar gaë . The Lālā sits and sits and swells out as he sits. 3. To be tired, or sick of ( uktānā ). 4. To growstrong, lusty, and mischievous. Bhīk ke ṭukṛe khā-ke aphrā hai . He fattens on the bread of charity. 5. To swagger; vaunt; show off; to be big; to look big or important. 6. To exceed bounds; run into excess; cut a dash. Phr. To give oneself airs. Muft kā rupayā milā, bas aphar gayā. aphar chal'nā , v. n. To strut about as proud as a peacock; to look big. Niklā paṛe hai jāme se kuchh in dinoṅ raqīb, Thoṛe se dam dilāse meṅ kitnā aphar chalā . Saudā. My rival from his shell has come out in these days. How swells and struts about this man on one false smile!
adj. Not frothy, without foam.
adj. (f. -ī), Very puffed up; self-conceited, opinionated; obstinate.

n. f. S. अहिफेन, P. shīr-i-khash khāsh , (idiom of Opium eaters) Chunnī Begam (little lady), Mah. Kālī Debī , (black goddess), Hin. Opium; the concrete juice of the Papaver somniferum . Wat. A fīm kahe, "maiṅ Chunnī Begam, mujh ko khā-ke jāë kahāṅ"? Song. Opium Says "I am Chunnī Begam , enjoy me, and you shall not leave me." — denā , v. a. To give opium; to poison; kill by poison. Nayā hakīm, de aphīm . Rus. Prov. A physician new with poison kills.
s.f. Opium.

v. a. 1. To stuff; cram; gorge; to feed one so that his belly swells out. Bahut guār de de-ke khaiṛe ko aphrā dȳā . Rus. 2. To give a person (money, etc.) to the utmost extent of his desire. aphrā'ī , n. f. aphā'rā , n. m. 1. A distention of the stomach from over feeding or drinking. 2. Surfeit; excess of food.

part. adj. (f. -ī), Swollen, puffed out; filled to overloading (the stomach); puffed up with pride, elated, &c. — aphrā jānā, v.n. To be or become glutted, cloyed, gorged, &c.; to be puffed up with pride; to be elated.
s.f. Surfeit, over-feeding, gorging; gluttony; distention of the stomach (from over-feeding); superfluity; superabundance, &c. (see aphrānā).
s. f. Gluttony, a surfeit.
v.t. To feed (one) till his stomach swells, to cram, glut, satiate; — to give one (money, &c.) to his heart's desire
more: (caus. of اپهرنا) v. a. To feed a person so that his belly swells; (met.) to give a person (money, &c.) to the utmost extent of his desire.

s. f. Opium.

adj. (f. -ā), Without pain.

adj. Unfit to be drunk, forbidden (liquor).

n. m. S. अप neg. यशस् fame. 1. Infamy; dishonor; disgrace; ignominy. Bārīre umiryā gaunā karaule, apne chalal pardeswā. Balamū, apjaswā lehela, nā? Mag. Wom. Song. Birah . What! leave a bride so young and go to foreign parts? Would you, my husband, bring a stain upon your name? 2. Defamation; calumny; obloquy; opprobium. 3. Disrepute; odium; scandal. Nāhaq apjas lȳā jagat meṅ, kyā khoṭī ā gaī ghaṛī! Song. Bārā-māsā . In vain disgrace you've brought upon yourself, an unlucky star presides.
(آپ Self, q. v. and कार्य्य Business) adj. Attending to own business, engaged in own affairs.
v.s.v. آپ āp.
more: n. m. One's own business; one's own affairs. Āp-kāj, mahā kāj . Prov. Self-done, well done. Also, One's own business is the most important. āp-kājī , adj. Selfish; self-seeking; minding one's own business; engaged in one's own affairs (khud garaz) . PHR. Looking after number one; taking care of oneself. āp (yā apne) ko dūr kheṅch'nā , v. n. To think a great deal of oneself. Kamīne ko ohdā milā, aur āp ko dūr kheṅchā . Put a low man in office and he'll fly sky high. āp hī āp, āpī āp , adv. Of himself, itself, etc.; alone. Is jaṅgal meṅ māī, na bāp, Alakh Niranjan āp hī āp . Within this jungle dense, nor mother, father his, Th' invisible, the passionless, alone exists. (Said of Shiva).

def: (آپ Self, q. v. and कार्य्य Business) adj. Attending to own business, engaged in own affairs.
s.m. Pouring upon; bathing, ablution.
s.m. Bathing, ablution; diving, submerging ( = āplāva, q.v.). s.m. Pouring upon; bathing, ablution.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Bathed, sprinkled, washed: — s.m. An initiated householder.

n. S. अप not, मन् to think Disrespect; slight; affront; insult; indignity; dishonor; disgrace; contempt. apmān' kar'nā , v. a. To degrade; debase; dishonor; humiliate. Bulāë-ke biḍārā, baṛ apmān kīnā . Rus. apmān' ho'nā , v. n. To be degraded, dishonored, insulted.
adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to self, own (one's, his, &c.
more: adj. 1. One's own; personal; own; belonging to one's self. 1. Apno apno hai, parāyo parāyo . Rus. Prov. Your own is your own, strangers are strangers. 2. Apno ghar, apno bāhar . Rus. Prov. Your house is my own, inside or out. 3. Dhī, jaṅvāī, bhāṅjā, yeh tīnoṅ nahīṅ apnā . Prov. A daughter, a son-in-law, and a nephew, these three are never one's own. 4. Apnā gū bhojan . Prov. One's own dirt is food. (One's own vices seem virtues). 2. Individual; private; peculiar.; appropriate; exclusive. Kām apnā hī kām hai . Prov. Self done is well done

n. One's family, friends, relatives, etc. 1. Apnā wohī jo apne kām āve . Prov. A friend in need is a friend indeed. 2. Hāë! jis kī khātir apnoṅ se bigāne ham bane, Sāf woh bhī le-ke dil apnā, begāna ban gayā . Jurat. Ah! she, for whom a stranger to my own I grew, Hath taken from me my heart and grown a stranger too.
m. اپني apnī, f. (see آپ āp ) pron. poss. Of or belonging to self, own. (In Braj, āpnau, āpne, āpnī ). اپني گاونا apnī-gāonā, To sing oneʼs own praises, to egotize.
subs: relatives, friends, &c — apnā apnā, adj. (f. -ī), Each his own; respective; particular, special; individual
To be keen each after his own interests or affairs
Each in its proper place; respective or assigned places
(or bigāna), s.m. Relations and strangers; friends and foes
adv. With a face peculiarly blank, or foolish-looking, or expressive of shame (e.g. apnā-sā muṅh le-kar āyā, 'he came with a peculiarly blank expression, or an expression of shame on his face')
To do all in one's power
On one's own part or side

or apnā kar-lenā, v.t. To make (a person or thing) one's own; to appropriate to one's use, possess oneself of, help oneself to; to usurp; to embezzle; to gain an ascendency over (a person), win (a person) over to one's side, attach (a person) to oneself, make (another) one's creature
adv. As, or as carefully as, one's own
To reap the fruit of one's own misdeeds, to reap what one has sown
To worship self, think much of oneself, to be vain or proud
To glory or revel in one's own means, to rejoice in one's independence; to be indifferent to fortune, to be independent
apnā kiyā pānā = apnī karnī bharnī
q.v. — apnī gānā, To sing one's own praises, blow one's own trumpet, to egotize, boast, brag; to be full of one's own affairs or grievances, &c.
To sleep as or when one pleases; to be one's own master; to be free from all care or anxiety
To dig one's own grave, be one's own destroyer or enemy
To chop off one's own hand, be one's own destroyer or enemy
To become one's own; to become attached to one; to become one's creature; to behave like a relation, act as one's own kindred.
ex: دوسروں کو دیکھنے سے پہلے اپنا خیال رکھو۔
dusrõ ko dekhne se pahle apnā xayāl rakho.
Before checking on the others make sure that you yourself are fine.
wety: Cognate with Southern Hindko اپڑا (apṛā) and Punjabi اپݨا (apṇā).

def: adj. 1. Each his own; respective; several; personal; particular; proper. Apnā apnā kamāna, apnā apnā khānā . Prov. 2. Allotted; due; just; appropriate. Apnā apnā laihnā hai . Prov. 3. Specific; individual; special. Apnā apnā ḍhaṅg hai . Each has its own special character. apnā sā mūṅh le-kar rah jānā . v. n. To hang down one's head, or hide one's face through shame. Sārī shahwat ṭkanḍī ho gaī, apnā sā mūṅh le-kar uth gayā . All his heat cooled down, and he rose up with his head in a bag. apnā kȳā pānā, apnī karnī bhar'nī yā bhog'nā , v. n. To suffer the consequences of one's own misdeeds; to reap what one has sown. Na nikleṅ kabhī chāo sāre tumhāre! Khudā hī se apnā kȳā pāo sāre! Garaz, ghāo par ghāo tum khāo sāre! Bichhaṛ jāo bakrī se, mar jāo sāre! Lage tum ko aisī hi golī, Kahāro . Wom. Raṇgīn. May unfulfilled your heart's desire remain! May God do unto you as you have done! In short, may wound on wound your portion be! May you, like goats dispersed, die, each and all! May Heaven deal such a blow to you, Kahārs! apnī apnī paṛnā . To look out, each for himself. āpnī apnī jagah . Each in its place; respective places; assigned or allotted places. apnī or nihār'. lit . Look to thyself. Regard thy own goodness. Bār barābar bār hai, tā par chalat byār, Raghubar, pār utārye, apnī or nihār! Dohā . The bridge a hair's breadth is, on it the wind doth blow, Oh Rām! take me across, thine own goodness regard! apne haq meṅ zahar kar'nā, yā kānṭe bo'nā . v. n. To put poison in one's own cup; to sow thistles; to injure oneself. Ai! le! afyūn khāī, qahr kȳā; Aur yeh apne haq meṅ zahr kȳā . Wom. Shauq. Well! well! opium you've taken, judgment brought upon your head, See now, another poison in your cup you've gone and mixed! apne ḍhā'ī chāṅ'val alag chhauṅk'nā yā galā'nā , v. n. 1. To have one's own way; to insist on. 2. To be meddlesome; to interfere, or meddle with. Koī sune yā na sune, ye apne ḍhāī chāṅval alag galātī hai . Wom. Whether any one listens or not, she will cook her own two and a half grains of rice all to her self, i. e. she will have her own say. apnī sī sab kuchh kar'nā . To do all one can; to do all that lies in one's power. apnī kar-ke na rakh'nā , v. a. 1. Not to keep as carefully as one's own. 2. To be come-at-able ( Slang ); to be ready to go with any one (a woman). Baṛī uchhāl-chhakkā haī, apnī kar-ke nahīṅ rakkhī . She is a bad one, she does n't keep it as her own apne ko mān'nā , v. n. To think much of oneself; to be vain of; to feel proud. Koī mȳān apne ko māne hai; Koī dās āp ko jāne hai . Mauj. Some lords esteem themselves; Some slaves regard themselves. apnī khāl meṅ mast ho'nā , v. n. To possess the sources of happiness with in oneself; to be indifferent to fortune; to be independent; to be content. apnī gā' nā , v. n. 1. To sing one's own praises; to be one's own trumpeter; to boast; brag; talk big. Phr. How we apples swim! Tum to sadā apnī hī gāte ho . 2. To be full of one's own affairs, distresses, etc.; to be always retailing one's own grievances. Garaz kā bāolā apnī gāwe . Prov. Mad of self, sings of self. apnī girah' yā gāṅṭh se , From one's own pocket. apnī guṛyā saṅwār denā , v. a. lit . To dress one's own doll. [Said of a father who defrays the expenses of his daughter's marriage, her dress, ornaments, etc. irrespective of what the bridegroom's family may spend]. apne muṅh , With one's own lips. Apne mūṅh mȳāṅ miṭṭhū . Prov. To be one's own trumpeter. apnī nīṅd so' nā , v. n. 1. To sleep when one pleases. 2. To be free from care or anxiety; to be one's own master; to be able to enjoy oneself. Apnī nīṅd sonā, apnī nīṅd uthnā . Prov. Sleep at pleasure, wake at pleasure. apnīoṅ par ā'nā, apnī wālīoṅ pe —, Wom. v. n. To be determined, or set upon; to resolve upon; to take into one's head; to be provoked; to be put on one's mettle. Jo maiṅ apnīoṅ par āūṅ, to kisī kī nā mānūṅ . Wom. If you once put me on my mettle, nothing shall stop me. apne hā'toṅ . adv. With one's own hands; himself. Apne hā'toṅ apnī izzat kho dī. apne hā'toṅ kulhāṛī mārnā,— —qabar khodnā , v. n. To be one's own destroyer or enemy.

n. m. 1. Relatives and strangers; relations and connections; friends and foes; every body. 1. Kahtī hai apne parāë se merā hāl tamām. Kyoṅ huī hai tū merī jān kī dushman, kokā? Wom. Rangīn. Wherefore my deadly foe, my foster sister, you've become? To everybody, near and far, my story you have told. 2. Apnā bigānā chhūṭā jāë! Bābal re, morā naīyar chhūṭā jāë! Song. ( Chāle kā gīt ). My earliest friends, mine own are passing far from me! Father! my childhood's home is passing quite from me! 3. Terī khātir ham ne, saīyāṅ, apnā bigāna chhāḍā . For your sake, my husband I have left my own. 2. The wife's and husband's relatives and connections; the whole family. ap'nā kar'nā,—kar le'nā, apnā'nā , v. a. 1. To make one's own; bring into one's own possession; occupy; secure; adopt. Bhāṅḍoṅ saṅg khetī kī, gā bajā ke apnī kī . Prov. (The husbandman) went shares in a field with some players—they sang and played and made it all their own. 2. To pocket; bag ( slang ); possess one's self of; make free with; help one's self to; usurp; embezzle; convert, or appropriate to one's own use; put into one's pocket. Unkī āṅkh band hote hī gairoṅ ne ghar apnā kar lȳā . 3. To bring under control, subjugation, etc. Thoṛe dinoṅ meṅ sārā mulk apnā kar lȳā . 4. To make friends with; to attach one's self to. Kisī ko apnā kar lo, yā kisī ke ho raho . Prov. 5. To charm; fascinate; captivate; enchant. Kāgā kā ko dhan hareṅ, aur koël kā ko deṅ, Yā jibhyā ke kārne jag apnā kar leṅ. Dohā . The crow doth rob no one, the cuckoo none makes It is her voice alone doth all the world bewitch.

v. n. 1. To become one's own; to own; to belong to; become the owner of. Kyā pardesī kī pīt, kyā phūs kā tāpnā! Dȳā kalejā kāṛh, huā nā āpnā . Prov. A straw-fire's heat, a stranger's love! I plucked my heart and gave it him, He never never was my own. 2. To possess (one's heart); to be mistress of; to gain one's affections. 3. To behave as one's own; to act like a relation. apne parā'ë kī ṭhok' reṅ khā' nā , v. n. To be set upon by the husband's relations. Paizār khāë apne parāë kī ṭhokreṅ . Wom. Bear the spurns of my husband's relations? Not I, nor my slippers for that matter. apne se , On ones own part or side.
(prov.) = اپنا apnā q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging to self, own (one's, his, &c.
subs: relatives, friends, &c — apnā apnā, adj. (f. -ī), Each his own; respective; particular, special; individual
To be keen each after his own interests or affairs
Each in its proper place; respective or assigned places
(or bigāna), s.m. Relations and strangers; friends and foes
adv. With a face peculiarly blank, or foolish-looking, or expressive of shame (e.g. apnā-sā muṅh le-kar āyā, 'he came with a peculiarly blank expression, or an expression of shame on his face')
To do all in one's power
On one's own part or side

or apnā kar-lenā, v.t. To make (a person or thing) one's own; to appropriate to one's use, possess oneself of, help oneself to; to usurp; to embezzle; to gain an ascendency over (a person), win (a person) over to one's side, attach (a person) to oneself, make (another) one's creature
adv. As, or as carefully as, one's own
To reap the fruit of one's own misdeeds, to reap what one has sown
To worship self, think much of oneself, to be vain or proud
To glory or revel in one's own means, to rejoice in one's independence; to be indifferent to fortune, to be independent
apnā kiyā pānā = apnī karnī bharnī
q.v. — apnī gānā, To sing one's own praises, blow one's own trumpet, to egotize, boast, brag; to be full of one's own affairs or grievances, &c.
To sleep as or when one pleases; to be one's own master; to be free from all care or anxiety
To dig one's own grave, be one's own destroyer or enemy
To chop off one's own hand, be one's own destroyer or enemy
To become one's own; to become attached to one; to become one's creature; to behave like a relation, act as one's own kindred.

def: v.t. To make (a thing, &c.) one's own, to convert to one's use, &c. ( = apnā karnā, q.v. s.v. apnā).
more: (see آپ āp ) v. a. To make a thing oneʼs own, to convert or appropriate to oneʼs own use.

def: = H اپنو अपनो, अपनौ apno, apnau, (Braj) = اپنا apnā, q.v.
s.f. Relationship, kinship; intimacy, friendship
more: n. f. Relationship; kinship; intimacy; friendship. Hamārī tumhārī apnāt kī sī bāt hai, tum se kyā nafa leṅge? We are friends-like. Would I make any profit of you?
(see آپ āp ) s. f. Family, relations, kindred, kin.

def: = H آپنو आपनौ āpnau, āpnauṅ, (Braj) = اپنا apnā, q.v.
s.f. Absence of splendour; darkness; dulness.
more: s. f. Want of splendour; darkness, dullness.

def: part. adj. Not current, not customary, not in vogue; obsolete.
adj. (f. -ā), Without dignity; — not strong, not violent, not formidable.
more: adj. Not violent, not strong or forcible, without dignity.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Modern, recent.

n. m. S. अप neg. राध् to accomplish. 1. Fault; transgression; offence; malpractice; misdemeanor. 2. Guilt; crime; delinquency; sin (pāp) . Baṛ aprādh kailan pāpī . Mag. This sinner hath a foul deed done. 3. Injury; wrong; violence; tyranny; excess. aprādh har'nā , v. a. To free from sin, vice, etc.; to grant salvation. Tulsī, saṅgat sādh kī harai koṭ aprādh . O Tulsī, the company of the good takes off a million of your faults. aprādh lagā'nā , v. a. 1. To charge; accuse; impeach. 2. To calumniate; slander; defame.
more: s. m. Offence, transgression, crime, fault.
ex: He was punished for committing the crimes.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Not chief, subordinate; powerless; little; mean.
s.m. April.
n. m. April.
adj. (f. -ā), Not visible, not manifest or evident; hidden, secret, private.
adj. (f. -ā), Unmanifested; not apparent.
adj. (t. -ā), Without proof; untrue.
adj. (f. -ā), Unauthentic, without authority.
s.m. Want of proof or of authority.

adj. Boundless; infinite. Khudyā lag rahī bahut, uṭhe haiṅ shole'se is tan meṅ; Māyā aprampār Rām kī, kyā kar ḍālā chhan meṅ! Play. Sarwar, Nīr. Sharp hunger gnaws me, flames of fire rise up within my frame, Boundless the might of Rām! See, in a second what a change!
adj. (f. -inī), Inanimate, lifeless.
part. Unobtained.
s.f. Non-attainment, non-acquisition.
part. adj. Unattainable.

ety: perhaps a corr. of A. ابرص abraṣ; but see the preceding
def: s.m. Leprosy.
n. m. Leprosy ( koṛ ).
s.m. Disapprobation, disfavour.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), That which is not presented to the Deity.
more: adj. That which is not presented to the deity.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Displeased, dissatisfied; disagreeable; unfavourable (to), averse (to); ungracious.
more: adj. Ungracious, unfavourable, displeased, dissatisfied, averse.

def: s.f. = S اپرسنتو अप्रसन्नत्व aprasannatwa, s.m. Displeasure, dissatisfaction, disfavour.
s.m. = S اپرسنتا अप्रसन्नता aprasannatā, s.f. Displeasure, dissatisfaction, disfavour.
adj. (f. -ā), Not celebrated; unknown; obscure; hidden. — aprasiddha-kartr̤ika, s.m. (in Gram.). The passive voice.
= अप्रत्यक्ष apratyaksh, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Imperceptible, invisible, not evident; not present, absent; secret.
more: from अ, प्रति and अक्षि Eye) adj. Imperceptible, not evident, secret, absent.

def: s.m. Want of brilliancy, or of acuteness, or power, or dignity, or fortune; dulness; bluntness; meanness.
more: s. m. Want of acuteness, of power or of fortune.

def: adj. (f. -inī), Dull; blunt; powerless; unfortunate; undignified; mean.
s.f. Deviation from custom; secrecy.
more: s. f. Deviation from custom: secrecy.

def: s.m. An undisputed inheritance.
part. adj. Uninterrupted, unhindered; unrepelled, unimpaired, uninjured.
s.f. Disregard, disesteem.
s.f. Want of respectability, ill-fame.
part. adj. Disgraced, dishonoured, disesteemed; unknown to fame, obscure.
adj. Without credit or confidence; unfriended; unworthy of confidence.
s.f. Want of confidence or credit, mistrust; unfriendliness.
s. f. Want of confidence or of credit.
adj. (f. -ā), Imperceptible, invisible, not evident; not present, absent; secret.
s.m. Distrust, doubt, unbelief, disbelief, mistrust.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Doubtful, mistrustful; sceptical; — doubter, sceptic.
adj. (f. -ā), Unskilful; inexperienced; stupid.
s.f. Want of experience; unskilfulness; stupidity.
s.m. Want of love, dislike, enmity, hatred.
s.f. Unfriendliness, dislike, enmity, hatred.
adj. (f. -ā), Unloved, disliked, hated; unlovable; unloving, unfriendly.
s. m. and adj. Self, self-formed, the deity.
adj. (dakh.) Extraordinary, rare, unique, choice, singular, strange, excellent, unequalled, exquisite.

adj. S. रूप् to form. 1. Self-formed; self-made or created. 2. Singular; strange; extraordinary; wonderful. 3. Rare; unique; choice; excellent; incomparable; unequalled; most beautiful; exquisite. Yeh laṛkā kaisā sundar āp-rūp hai! āp-ṛūp , n. m. 1. The self-formed; self; the Deity. Sākshāt Āp-Rūp ā gaë . 2. You; yourself; your honor ( Khud ba-daulat ); your ladyship. Gar āp-rūp ham se bātoṅ meṅ ṭuk kaṛe hoṅ, Sau ragṛe, jhagṛe, qazye, qisse jhaṭ uṭh khaṛe hoṅ . Inshā. If harsh in slightest sort to me your ladyship should speak, A hundred words, disputes, and quarrels, tales would quickly spring. 3. Ironically , Scamp; imp; little devil; His Majesty. Dekha, to āp-rūp brājmān haīṅ! āp-rū'pī , adv. Of its own accord; spontaneously. Mātā maīyā āp-rūpī bhailthī . Mag. Wom.
s. (dakh.) Rarity, curiosity, fineness ( Harris ).
s.f. A female dancer in the court of Indra; a nymph; a courtesan. — apsarā-swar, s.m. The notes or echoes of the apsarās.
more: s. f. A female dancer or courtezan in Swarga or paradise.

def: n. f. See apchharā .

adj. Selfish; self-seeking. āp se, āp se āp , adv. Of himself; of itself; sui motu; ex mero motu; of his own accord; spontaneously; without any reason or cause; designedly; intentionally; wilfully; advisedly; voluntarily; of one's own accord. Lugāī rahe to āp se, nahīṅ jāë sage bāp se . Prov. If a woman will stay in the house, stay she will: If not, her own father can't keep her. Or thus— If a woman is chaste, chaste she will be; if unchaste, she will go with her own father. āp se ā' nā , v. n. 1. To come of one's own accord or unexpectedly; to flow spontaneously. 1. Āp se āwe, to āne de . Prov. [The story is told of the wife of a strict Mahomedan recounting to him with great glee how she had captured a fat capon for his dinner. The pious man was greatly shocked, and desired her to throw away the unlawful thing. But the thrifty housewife remonstrating that she had spent a deal of ghee and spices on the meal, his scruples were so far overcome that he consented to partake of the gravy only. Accordingly, in deference to the good man's scruples, every bit of meat that came along with the gravy was being carefully put back, when he cried out, in the words of the proverb—"What comes of itself, let it come."] 2. Kahā ki "ham nahīṅ āne ke yāṅ." To us ne, "Nazīr," Kahā ki "socho to, kyā āp se tum āte ho ?" Nazīr. I said. "No more will I come here." Then, O Nazir, she said, "Bethink you now if of yourself you do indeed come here." 2. To come uninvited; to drop in. Koī āp se ātā hai?
adj. (f. -ā.), Authentic, true, trustworthy.
s.f. A quotient; an equation of a degree.
adj. (f. -ā), Tail-less.
s.f. The tree Dalbergia sisn.
(for अ and पूज्य To be worshipped) adj. That which is not worshipped, or is not a proper object of worship.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Irreverent, irreligious; not deserving reverence (a god), not deserving respect (a man); — an irreligious person, &c.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Not to be worshipped or revered; unlawful or unsuitable as an object of worship.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Unclean, unholy; irreligious; void of merit; wicked; — unholiness; irreligiousness, wickedness.
= اپورو apūrva, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Unprecedented, unparalleled, unheard of, uncommon, remarkable, extraordinary, wonderful; eminent; new, strange.
more: comp. of अ and पूर्व Preceding) adj. New, unheard of, admirable, uncommon, remarkable, unparalleled.

def: = اپوروي apūrvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Unprecedented, &c. ( = apūrva, q.v.); — an eminent or an uncommon person.
